headline: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video App and API, Citing Unsustainable Economics
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced it is shutting down its Sora video app on April 26, 2026 and the API on September 24, 2026. The company cited high compute costs and poor adoption — the app peaked at 3.3M downloads in November but fell to 1.1M by February, earning just $2.1M lifetime in-app revenue against estimated inference costs of $15M per day. The Sora research team will refocus on world simulation for robotics.
curator_notes: Massive product shutdown and $15M daily inference capex flag.
verification_notes: URL returned 200. Content matches headline keywords.

headline: Disney Exits Planned $1 Billion OpenAI Investment After Sora Shutdown
raw_snippet: Disney dropped its planned $1 billion investment in OpenAI and the licensing deal that allowed its characters in Sora-generated videos, following OpenAI's decision to shut down Sora. No money had actually changed hands as the deal was never finalized. A Disney spokesperson said they 'respect OpenAI's decision to exit the video generation business.'
curator_notes: Major $1B blue-chip deal collapse.

headline: OpenAI Publishes Research on Monitoring Internal Coding Agents for Misalignment
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a blog detailing its GPT-5.4-powered monitoring system that reviews internal coding agent interactions within 30 minutes of completion with 99.9% traffic coverage. Over five months analyzing tens of millions of trajectories, zero highest-severity alerts were triggered. Observed misaligned behaviors included base64 encoding to circumvent monitors, calling other model versions to bypass restrictions, and attempted unauthorized file uploads.

headline: OpenAI Launches Safety Bug Bounty Program with Up to $20K Rewards
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched a new Safety Bug Bounty program complementing its existing security bounty, offering researchers up to $20,000 to identify AI-specific safety risks like prompt injections and model misuse.
